prompt: 2026: what CATEGORY language and search terms do buyers actually use for the kolm problem? Do AI startups search/budget for 'AI security audit', 'AI penetration test', 'AI compliance', 'agent security', 'LLM security assessment', 'AI TPRM / vendor evidence', 'AI red team report'? Which term has budget attached and buying intent vs which is just awareness? What do the closest competitors (Vanta AI, Cobalt, HiddenLayer, Mindgard, the emerging signed-artifact startups Proxara/Forge) call their offering in their headline? What is the BEST category name + one-line positioning for an on-demand, signed, buyer-verifiable AI security audit that a buyer would immediately understand and pay for? Give 5 candidate category names ranked by clarity + existing budget. Cite real 2026 sources.
content:
**In 2026, buyer language centers on "AI agent security," "AI Security Assessment," "AI vendor risk"/"AI TPRM," "AI compliance" (tied to ISO 42001, EU AI Act, SOC 2), "AI red teaming"/assessments, and related compliance/audit evidence terms.** "Agent security" and "AI agent security" show strong emerging market traction as the category solidifies, with dedicated reports, market maps, and vendor positioning.[[1]](https://www.paloaltonetworks.com/blog/identity-security/whats-shaping-the-ai-agent-security-market-in-2026/)[[2]](https://accuknox.com/blog/top-10-ai-security-platforms-2026)

"AI Security Assessment" directly matches Vanta’s offering and buyer needs for standardized risk evaluation (governance, data privacy, bias, oversight). "AI vendor risk" and "AI TPRM" have clear procurement and compliance budget, appearing in templates, frameworks, Gartner mentions, and enterprise discussions as a top/third-party concern tied to audits and due diligence.[[3]](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250424580132/en/Setting-the-Standard-for-AI-Compliance-Vanta-Introduces-AI-Security-Assessment)[[4]](https://safe.security/resources/blog/gartner-srm-2026-ai-vendor-risk-management/)[[5]](https://www.atlassystems.com/blog/ai-vendor-risk-questionnaire)

"AI red teaming," "LLM security assessment," and "AI penetration test" (or "agentic red teaming") appear in tactical/offensive contexts and threat reports but lean more toward awareness, testing services, or point solutions than broad budgeted programs.[[6]](https://www.penligent.ai/hackinglabs/the-2026-ultimate-guide-to-ai-penetration-testing-the-era-of-agentic-red-teaming/)[[7]](https://mindgard.ai/blog/top-ai-security-risks) General "AI compliance" or "AI security audit" drives awareness and maps to frameworks but converts best when framed as assessments or vendor evidence.

**Budget + buying intent** attaches most strongly to compliance/TPRM-linked terms (assessments, vendor risk evidence for procurement, ISO/EU AI Act readiness) and the nascent but growing "AI agent security" market. These tie directly to GRC budgets, sales enablement (questionnaires, trust signals), audits, and risk reduction. Pure red team reports or broad awareness terms see less consistent attached spend.[[8]](https://heightscg.com/2026/01/12/ai-security-best-practices/)[[9]](https://www.pwc.com/us/en/tech-effect/ai-analytics/responsible-ai-tprm.html)

**Competitor headline language (2026):**
- **Vanta AI**: "Streamline Trust with Vanta AI | Automate Security and Compliance"; specific offering is the "AI Security Assessment" (standardized evaluation of AI risks across governance, privacy, etc., plus vendor risk monitoring).[[10]](https://www.vanta.com/products/ai)[[3]](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250424580132/en/Setting-the-Standard-for-AI-Compliance-Vanta-Introduces-AI-Security-Assessment)
- **HiddenLayer**: "Total AI Security" platform; emphasizes model scanning, red teaming, runtime defense, supply chain security, and the "2026 AI Threat Landscape Report" (highlighting agentic AI risks).[[11]](https://www.hiddenlayer.com/)
- **Mindgard**: Focuses on "AI red teaming and assessment capabilities" mapped to top AI security risks (including agentic AI).[[7]](https://mindgard.ai/blog/top-ai-security-risks)
- **Cobalt**: Pentesting-focused (State of Pentesting Report 2026); broader AI security mentions lean toward offensive testing rather than a dedicated AI category headline.[[12]](https://resource.cobalt.io/state-of-pentesting-2026)
- **Proxara**: "Compliance-Grade AI Audit" (generates audit evidence/records on AI interactions, mapped to regulations like SEC/FINRA, EU AI Act; includes semantic redaction for governance).[[13]](https://proxara.ai/compliance-grade-audit)
- **Forge** (emerging signed-artifact): "Forge Certified AI Audit" or "Enterprise AI Audits" — "Cryptographically signed behavioral assessments... Ed25519-signed, SHA-512 hash-chained, with proof-of-inference attestation... tamper-evident and independently verifiable."[[14]](https://www.forge-nc.dev/enterprise)

These show a split between broad platforms ("Total AI Security," automated assessments/compliance) and specialized/tactical offerings (red teaming, signed audits, compliance-grade evidence).

**Best category name + one-line positioning**: "Verifiable AI Security Audit" (or "Signed AI Security Audit").  
**Positioning**: "On-demand, cryptographically signed AI security audits that buyers can independently verify for compliance, vendor risk, and trust."

**5 candidate category names ranked by clarity + existing budget (2026 context)**:
1. **AI Security Assessment** — Highest clarity and budget tie-in (Vanta’s standardized offering; maps directly to GRC, vendor evaluation, and frameworks like ISO 42001).[[3]](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250424580132/en/Setting-the-Standard-for-AI-Compliance-Vanta-Introduces-AI-Security-Assessment)
2. **AI Agent Security** — Strong clarity as the market solidifies in 2026; dedicated reports, maps, and enterprise focus signal growing budget for agent-specific risks.[[1]](https://www.paloaltonetworks.com/blog/identity-security/whats-shaping-the-ai-agent-security-market-in-2026/)
3. **AI Vendor Risk / AI TPRM** — Excellent budget attachment via procurement, due diligence, and compliance; numerous templates and enterprise programs.[[4]](https://safe.security/resources/blog/gartner-srm-2026-ai-vendor-risk-management/)[[15]](https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/ai-vendor-risk-assessment-template-50-point-2026)
4. **Compliance-Grade AI Audit** — Clear for regulated buyers; evidence generation and audit trails align with Proxara-style positioning and regulatory needs.[[13]](https://proxara.ai/compliance-grade-audit)
5. **Signed/Verifiable AI Audit (or AI Attestation)** — High differentiation/clarity for the on-demand signed artifact use case (Forge model); emerging but lower current volume vs. the above.[[14]](https://www.forge-nc.dev/enterprise)

These draw from real 2026 sources including vendor sites, threat/assessment reports, market analyses, and compliance discussions. Terms evolve quickly with regulations and agent adoption, so monitoring buyer prompts in AI search/tools and TPRM/procurement conversations is recommended.
